factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY trades firmly around 98.50 per FXStreet, maintaining strength but lacking momentum into Trump-Xi summit. USD upside may be capped pending trade/tariff outcomes.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Not explicitly mentioned in news, but elevated nominal yields and sticky inflation expectations suggest real yields remain elevated, which is structurally headwindy for gold.

factors.name: US Inflation Data (PPI)
factors.summary: US PPI beat expectations (described as 'hot US PPI' by FXStreet), reinforcing expectations for sticky inflation and higher-for-longer rates. This supports real yields and undermines gold near-term.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit underway with market caution prevalent; Iran war ongoing (Hormuz disruptions, OPEC demand cuts). Safe-haven bid for gold should be present, but offset by cautious positioning ahead of outcome.

factors.name: Fed Policy Outlook
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh confirmed as next Fed chair (most divisive vote on record), but no immediate FOMC decision or new dot plot in this window. Fed appears on hold; no dovish catalyst evident.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI slides below $97 to ~$96.80 per FXStreet, despite Iran war and Hormuz concerns. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations, which removes upside pressure on gold and supports real yields.

factors.name: Yuan (CNY/CNH) Strength
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY at 6.8401 vs estimate 6.7888 (gap ~540 pips, largest since March 2), while CNH hit strongest vs USD since Feb 2023. Stronger yuan messaging to Trump may reduce trade escalation fears, lowering safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Gold Positioning / Technicals
factors.summary: Gold 'holds steady' and trades 'on a flat note' per FXStreet; silver halted 6-day winning streak. No breakout directional conviction; markets awaiting Trump-Xi summit conclusion.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh PBoC, RBI, or other CB buying mentioned in this news cycle; structural bid remains but not actively signaled.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: EUR/USD recovers above 1.1700 on hawkish ECB signals per FXStreet; higher European rates relative to US could support DXY weakness and gold upside if dollar falters, but also reflects wider rate differentials (mixed).

screening.narrative: Gold is stuck in a holding pattern as the Trump-Xi summit dominates risk sentiment and geopolitical headlines. While the Iran war and Hormuz disruptions should underpin safe-haven demand, recent hot US PPI data reinforces sticky inflation and higher-for-longer rate expectations, supporting real yields and capping gold upside. The PBOC's yuan-friendly fixing suggests de-escalation messaging to Trump, which could reduce geopolitical risk premium. With markets in full-on summit watch mode and no major Fed/CPI catalyst for 24-48h, conviction is low; gold could break decisively once summit outcome becomes clear.
catalysts_ahead: Trump-Xi summit conclusion/readout (likely next 12-24h with trade/tariff details)
UK GDP data (Q1 2026 preliminary, expected 0.6% growth)
UK Industrial & Manufacturing Production (today/tomorrow)
Possible Fed speakers or commentary (Kevin Warsh confirmation implications)
Oil market reaction to Hormuz transit updates and OPEC demand cuts
Any further CNY strength/PBOC signaling ahead of trade talks
screening.risk_warning: A dovish Trump-Xi outcome (tariff rollback, trade de-escalation) would lower geopolitical premium and safe-haven demand, pressuring gold further. Conversely, hawkish trade rhetoric or Hormuz military escalation could trigger a sharp gold rally. Hot inflation surprises (e.g., PCE next week) would support higher real yields and USD, both bearish gold. If US equities rally hard on summit euphoria, gold could underperform risk assets despite headline safety concerns.

screening.current_price: ~2330-2350 (estimated; directionally mixed, edges higher per FXStreet report)

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY holds firmly near 98.50 during Asian hours. Stronger USD typically inverse to gold; however, momentum is being tempered by weaker USD/CNY (yuan at 3-month highs) due to Trump-Xi diplomacy optics.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS yield data in this news window. Nominal 10y Treasury yields not explicitly quoted; US nominal rates appear stable. Without real-yield directional clarity, this critical gold driver remains unanchored.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tension (Iran War / Middle East)
factors.summary: Iran-US war continues to disrupt trade and energy markets. References to Strait of Hormuz transits, supertanker exits, and oil supply risks all support safe-haven demand for gold. Oil remains elevated (WTI ~$96.80, slipping but still volatile).

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI / Brent)
factors.summary: WTI slipped below $97 for second straight day per late Asian session data; IEA warning on 'greater volatility ahead' and OPEC cutting 2026 demand to 1.2M bpd/d suggests supply concerns are easing slightly, reducing immediate inflation pressures.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent US CPI/PPI)
factors.summary: News references 'hotter-than-expected US inflation' supporting USD earlier vs AUD/USD. Specific CPI/PPI figures not detailed in this batch, but tone suggests recent inflation prints were on the hot side, likely supporting Fed hawkishness and real yields.

factors.name: ECB Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: ECB officials signaling hawkish bias (Kazaks: will hike if oil deanchors inflation; Breeden: BoE cannot wait forever but not June/July imminent). However, Bloomberg notes June ECB hike becoming 'less certain.' Mixed messaging reduces clarity, but hawkish tone overall supports EUR strength and complicates gold's funding.

factors.name: BoJ & Yen Dynamics
factors.summary: BoJ policymaker Masu warns Iran war shock could exceed 1973 oil crisis impact; yen depreciation raising inflation expectations. Japan mulling supplementary budget. Suspected $65B FX intervention has shifted yen from undervalued to overvalued (150-160 USD/JPY floor defense). Higher USD/JPY ordinarily bullish gold, but BoJ tightening bias and market intervention reduce yen carry trade appeal.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit (Live, outcomes TBD)
factors.summary: High-stakes talks began Thursday covering trade, tariffs, technology, Taiwan, Iran. Yuan strengthening ahead of talks suggests de-escalation hopes. Outcome unknown; markets in 'wait-and-see' mode. Tariff deal optimism could weaken safe-haven demand; conflict escalation would support gold.

factors.name: UK GDP Beat
factors.summary: UK Q1 GDP +0.6% vs +0.6% expected; March +0.3% vs -0.2% expected. Solid growth data typically reduces gold's appeal (supports GBP, reduces BoE urgency to cut), but impact on gold is marginal given UK-specific news and broader macro.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Demand
factors.summary: Equities trading mixed in Asia ahead of Xi-Trump summit. Tech stocks (Nvidia, AI infrastructure) rallying on earnings, but caution prevails. Gold edges higher as traders await summit outcome; safe-haven bid present but not overwhelming.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows / Central Bank Demand
factors.summary: No explicit GLD, IAU, or PBoC gold-buying data in this window. Chinese supertanker exiting Hormuz after months stranded suggests some normalization of Middle East supply, but no direct CB gold purchasing signals.

screening.narrative: Gold trades slightly higher during Asian/early European session on geopolitical safe-haven bid (Iran war, Middle East tensions) and dovish-leaning ECB/BoE signals, but momentum is constrained by firmer USD (DXY ~98.50) and evidence of recent hot US inflation supporting Fed hawkishness. Trump-Xi summit underway in Beijing creates headline risk and uncertainty—markets treading water for outcomes. Oil slightly weaker (WTI ~$96.80, down from $97+) suggests inflation pressures may be easing, which could pressure real yields lower (gold-supportive) or higher if recession fears rise (USD-supportive, gold-bearish). Overall: no clear consensus; traders balanced on a knife-edge waiting for summit signals and upcoming US macro data.
catalysts_ahead: Trump-Xi summit outcomes (Thursday-Friday, May 14-15): tariffs, trade deal risk, Taiwan, Iran policy clarity
US Retail Sales data (likely Friday or early next week): consumer strength indicator
ECB/BoE speakers and guidance: inflation/rate-hike expectations (Kazaks, Breeden comments in window; more expected)
OPEC + IEA energy forecasts: oil price direction and inflation input
Potential FOMC decision or Powell speech if scheduled (not mentioned in this window, but high-frequency Fed messaging possible)
screening.risk_warning: If Trump-Xi summit yields tariff truce and de-escalation rhetoric, risk-on sentiment could spike equities and weaken safe-haven gold demand, especially if USD strengthens further. Conversely, if summit talks fail or escalate tensions (Taiwan, Iran), gold would likely rally sharply. Fed hawkishness—evidenced by hot inflation data—could drive real yields higher and USD strength, both bearish for gold. Any sharp move in oil (up or down) will also reprrice near-term inflation expectations and Treasury yields. Monitor DXY (key inverse), 10y yield trajectory, and headline/summit developments closely over next 48-72 hours; high event risk makes conviction risky.

factors.name: Real Yields / US Treasury Yields
factors.summary: US inflation surprises (PPI stronger than expected per MUFG/FXStreet) have driven hawkish Fed repricing and higher Treasury yields. OCBC notes hot CPI/PPI pushed yields higher, though much inflation risk is already priced. Rising nominal yields, if real yields follow, headwind for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Stance
factors.summary: Fed sentiment shifting hawkish: multiple policymakers abandoning easing bias per Investinglive. Stronger-than-expected inflation data triggering repricing of rate-cut expectations lower. This tightens financial conditions, typical headwind for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY gaining for fourth consecutive day, near 98.50 per FXStreet, supported by hawkish repricing. USD/JPY climbing toward prior highs (158.00 area). Stronger dollar typically inverse to gold—short-term pressure on XAUUSD.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Data
factors.summary: April PPI stronger than expected; Franklin Templeton CEO warns markets underestimating inflation stickiness. Spain inflation still elevated due to energy. However, no new CPI headline in this window—last CPI appears to have been the catalyst. Energy-driven inflation may support gold as hedge, but if seen as persistent, Fed stays hawkish (negative for gold via real yields).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk / Oil Prices
factors.summary: UK ship seized in Strait of Hormuz, Iran conflict on day 76, Cuba oil embargo hitting supplies. WTI ~$96.70, IEA flagging 'greater volatility ahead.' Geopolitical risk premium supports safe-haven demand for gold, though supply risks also support oil. Mixed net effect but a real floor under gold.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit (De-escalation Risk-Off)
factors.summary: Trump-Xi meeting 'good' per White House; both agreed Strait of Hormuz must remain open, discussed economic cooperation, US oil sales to China. Positive headlines reduce geopolitical premium; offshore yuan posting best win streak since 2017. Risk-on tone favors USD over gold.

factors.name: China Credit / Economic Weakness
factors.summary: China new bank loans came in at -¥10 billion in April; year-to-date lending only ¥8.59 trillion. Credit contraction and weak demand could slow global growth, supporting safe-haven demand for gold. Long-term negative for equities; net supportive for precious metals.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No new PBoC, RBI, or other CB gold purchase news in this window. India doubled gold/silver tariffs to 15% (per Commerzbank) to support rupee and FX reserves amid Iran conflict—structural support but already market-priced.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows / Positioning
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU holdings data in this news window. Silver diverging from gold and rallying (XAG/USD near $87, failing to break $90 per FXStreet), suggests selective precious metals buying; gold positioning unclear.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equities
factors.summary: Dow Jones futures +0.30%, Nasdaq futures +0.41%, Asian equities rallying on tech strength (Cisco +17% on AI, Nokia jump). Positive risk sentiment is typically headwind for gold. However, geopolitical premium and China growth concerns cap upside risk-on.

factors.name: Other Central Banks (BoE, ECB, BoJ)
factors.summary: UK political crisis (Starmer rivals planning leadership bids) threatens bond market stability; GBP pressured. BoJ yen intervention fading (USD/JPY strength). No fresh ECB/BoE/BoJ policy news; no immediate dovish surprises that would support gold.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Complex
factors.summary: WTI down slightly to $96.70, but supply risks (Hormuz, Iran war, Cuba embargo) and IEA warning of higher volatility ahead keep oil supported. Energy inflation remains elevated; supports gold as inflation hedge.

screening.narrative: Gold faces a tactical headwind from hawkish Fed repricing (hot US inflation data, PPI surprise) and a strengthening dollar (DXY +4 days, USD/JPY near 158). However, offsetting tailwinds include persistent geopolitical risk (Hormuz seizure, Iran conflict day 76), China's weak credit data signaling growth slowdown, and energy inflation remaining elevated. The Trump-Xi summit de-escalation narrative has dented the geopolitical risk premium in the short term, favoring equities and USD over safe-haven gold. Real yields are likely rising, the primary headwind; positioning appears mixed with silver diverging bullishly from gold.
catalysts_ahead: US Retail Sales (May 14–15 release window, likely Thursday AM)
Further Trump-Xi summit headlines (ongoing through Friday)
ECB commentary (Lagarde mentioned in context of Euro moves)
Next FOMC decision date confirmation (dot plot hawkish repricing underway)
Any new Iran conflict escalation or Hormuz closure threat
Oil market reaction to IEA volatility warning
screening.risk_warning: If US Treasury real yields break sharply higher (nominal yields up + inflation expectations down) OR if Trump-Xi summit produces durable de-escalation + trade truce (removing geopolitical premium), gold could test support below 2320. Conversely, if Hormuz tensions flare again or China recession fears spike, gold rallies past 2400. Equity momentum and DXY strength are near-term headwinds; watch for any dovish Fed pivot or inflation data miss to flip bias bullish.

factors.name: US Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No new TIPS data in the news window. Long-end nominal yields remain elevated amid inflation concerns (Middle East shock, energy prices), but no clear directional shift reported. Real yields likely still elevated, which is headwind for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy & Expectations
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair in narrowest Senate vote ever (per Bloomberg 5/14 11:30), but no new FOMC decision or dot plot in this window. Franklin Templeton CEO noted markets may be underestimating inflation risk, suggesting some Fed tightening bias persists.

factors.name: BoJ Hawkish Tilt & Yen Carry
factors.summary: MUFG reports rising JGB yields and hawkish BoJ commentary driving near-term rate hike expectations (per FXStreet 5/14 11:49). Tightening BoJ could unwind yen carry trades, supporting USD/JPY higher and potentially benefiting gold as carry unwind triggers risk-off.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: BBH notes DXY consolidating near 200-day MA within 96.00–100.00 range (5/14 10:01). Mixed signals from Trump-Xi summit (warmer rhetoric on trade) may weigh on dollar strength near-term, though fiscal risks linger. AUD down 0.25% and underperforming despite positive summit outcome.

factors.name: Geopolitical: Trump-Xi Summit & Taiwan
factors.summary: Trump-Xi met in Beijing with 'cordial' tone on trade (large Boeing orders expected, soybean purchases discussed), but Xi warned Trump on Taiwan (per Bloomberg 5/14 10:54). Mixed signal: risk-off from Taiwan tensions offset by near-term trade optimism. No immediate escalation.

factors.name: Geopolitical: Middle East & Oil
factors.summary: Ongoing Iran-US tensions persist; WTI around $97.30 (0.34% up), IEA warns of persistent supply deficit (5/14 10:59). Elevated energy inflation supports gold as inflation hedge; however, no new escalation reported. Ukraine conflict also ongoing (Russian strikes on Kyiv, 5/14 11:46).

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Prints
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in last 24h. MUFG cites inflation risks from Middle East conflict. ECB's Kazaks (5/14 09:33) notes inflation likely to remain elevated for some time and stagflation not baseline. Mixed: expectations remain sticky but no new surprise in window.

factors.name: China Credit & Economic Weakness
factors.summary: China new bank loans came in at –¥10 billion in April (another negative reading, 5/14 09:31). Sluggish credit demand and weak borrowing conditions undermine risk appetite and commodity demand, headwind for gold as risk asset.

factors.name: Safe-Haven Flows & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Equity futures upbeat (S&P, Nasdaq at new highs, Cisco soars on AI earnings). Tech rally persists; risk sentiment improving on summit optimism. Gold trading flat around $4,700, suggesting investors rotating to risk assets rather than safe havens. No VIX spike or credit stress.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No new CB gold purchases reported in this 24h window. Structural bid from PBoC, RBI, Turkey remains, but no fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows (GLD, IAU)
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU holdings data mentioned in news window. Unable to assess institutional positioning shift.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a consolidation near $4,700 amid mixed macro signals. The Trump-Xi summit has eased near-term US-China trade tensions, supporting equities and risk appetite—a headwind for gold. However, persistent Middle East tensions (elevated oil around $97.30), hawkish BoJ rhetoric, and sticky inflation expectations provide structural support for bullion. The real yield backdrop remains unclear without fresh TIPS data, but nominal yields staying elevated act as a drag. China's weak April credit growth (-¥10B new loans) adds deflationary undertone, further pressuring the case for higher gold.
catalysts_ahead: US Retail Sales (May 14–15 likely release or expectation)
Fed Warsh confirmation speech/commentary (new chair honeymoon period)
ECB meeting/guidance (Kazaks reiterates data-dependent approach; next rate call TBD)
BoJ April Summary of Deliberations and next hike timing (market pricing for near-term tightening)
Iran-US escalation watch (Strait of Hormuz risk; oil supply/inflation impact)
China stimulus or credit data updates (credit impulse critical for commodity demand)
Treasury yield moves (if nominal yields roll over sharply, real yields could compress—bullish gold)
screening.risk_warning: If equities continue to rally on Trump-Xi optimism and risk sentiment strengthens, gold could break lower toward $4,650–$4,600 as investors exit safe havens. Conversely, if real yields spike (TIPS higher on inflation shock or Fed hawkishness), gold downside accelerates. Upside invalidation: a sharp Middle East escalation, a sudden BoJ pivot to easing (softening yen strength narrative), or a China credit impulse surprise could reignite gold's bull case past $4,750–$4,800.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields rising as hawkish Fed repricing occurs; Bloomberg reports 'hawkish Fed repricing boosts Treasury yields' on Thursday. Higher nominal yields, unless inflation expectations collapse, imply higher real yields, which is inversely correlated with gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD strength implied by hawkish repricing and geopolitical safety bid. AUDUSD breaking below 200-hour MA, USDCAD breaking above 100-day MA — both show USD gaining. Stronger USD is bearish for gold in USD terms.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Stance
factors.summary: Multiple references to Fed policymakers abandoning easing bias; FXStreet notes 'hawkish Fed repricing' driving yields higher. No dovish pivot signaled; rates expected to remain higher for longer per inflation commentary.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent Prints)
factors.summary: US import prices +1.9% vs +1.0% expected and export prices +3.3% vs +1.1% expected (per Investinglive, May 14). CPI still 'higher for longer' per MarketWatch. Hot inflation surprises initially pressure gold by supporting hawkish rate expectations.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Middle East / Iran-US)
factors.summary: Iran-US conflict appears 'temporarily on hold,' easing anxiety per Investinglive. Oil trading ~$100 as tensions ease; floating armoury seizure reported but no escalation noted. Risk premium fading, which removes a bullish driver for gold as safe-haven.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Crude ~$99-100, down ~$0.70 on day as geopolitical tensions ease (Investinglive). While TD Securities warns Brent could spike to $150 if supply risks materialize, current calm is removing near-term inflation/risk drivers supporting gold.

factors.name: Equity/Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures +0.3%, Nasdaq extending gains, Cisco soaring, AI momentum strong (Bloomberg). Trump-Xi summit positive optics ('good meeting' per White House). Risk-on environment competes with safe-haven gold demand.

factors.name: Gold Positioning & Flows
factors.summary: No mention of GLD/IAU flows or central bank buying in news window. Physical seasonality (Indian, Chinese) not active in May. ETF positioning unclear.

factors.name: Other Central Banks (BoJ, ECB)
factors.summary: BoJ hawkish commentary pushing JPY higher; MUFG notes rising JGB yields reinforce expectations for near-term BoJ rate hike. ECB slowly hiking. Global rate expectations rising, which supports USD and pressures gold.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit Optics
factors.summary: All smiles, Xi warns about Taiwan but no immediate escalation. Bessent says US 'in the lead' on AI talks. Softer US-China rhetoric is removing geopolitical risk premium but also supporting risk appetite, which is mixed for gold.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a tug-of-war between hawkish Fed repricing (pushing real yields higher and USD stronger) and fading geopolitical risk premium as Iran-US tensions ease and Trump-Xi optics remain cordial. Hot inflation surprises in US import/export prices on May 14 support continued Fed hawkishness and higher-for-longer rate narrative, which is bearish for real yields and gold. Equity momentum remains strong (Cisco, AI, S&P 500 records), reducing safe-haven demand. The dominant theme is rising real yields + stronger USD + reduced risk premium, all headwinds for gold, but the absence of any Fed decision or major macro surprise today keeps the market in a holding pattern ahead of larger catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: US Producer Price Index (PPI) print — likely next business day, could confirm inflation stickiness
University of Michigan Inflation Expectations survey — key for breakeven tracking
Next FOMC decision/Powell testimony — timing unclear from news, but hawkish bets building
China-US trade/tariff developments from Trump-Xi summit (end of Friday per timeline)
Oil supply shock risk from Middle East or geopolitical escalation — TD Securities warns Brent upside to $150
screening.risk_warning: If US inflation data turns cooler than expected or Fed pivots dovish, or if geopolitical tensions (Iran, Taiwan, Russia-Ukraine) suddenly escalate sharply, gold could rally despite current headwinds. Conversely, if equity markets stall or credit stress emerges, USD safe-haven demand could offset real yield headwinds. Confidence is capped at 5 because major data (CPI/PCE) and potential Fed signals are imminent, and markets typically chop into events rather than break decisively.

factors.name: US Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD rallying sharply on resilient US retail sales (April held at consensus) and hawkish Fed commentary. EUR/USD down 3 days, GBP/USD under pressure, AUD/USD breaking 200-hour MA—classic broad dollar strength regime.

factors.name: Hawkish Fed outlook
factors.summary: Multiple Fed speakers (Pill, Schmid) emphasizing inflation remains 'too high' and second-round effects are risk; no pivot to easing signaled. Kansas City Fed's Schmid: continued inflation is 'most pressing risk.' Kevin Warsh taking over chair—uncertainty on stance but current messaging is hawkish.

factors.name: Real yields (implied)
factors.summary: US nominal yields holding firm amid inflation concerns and delayed easing expectations. With inflation recelerating (April CPI/PPI both reaccelerating per UOB report), real yields likely rising or staying elevated, which is BEARISH for gold.

factors.name: Oil & energy dynamics
factors.summary: Iran conflict ongoing, Hormuz supertanker flows creeping higher; energy inflation persists. This supports inflation expectations but also marginalizes Fed near-term cut odds, which helps USD and pressures gold.

factors.name: US economic resilience
factors.summary: Retail sales strong (3rd month up), business inventories beat, S&P 500 and Nasdaq at records, Dow futures above 50k. Risk-on sentiment and strong growth narrative reduce safe-haven demand for gold; equities are outperforming.

factors.name: Geopolitical safe-haven bid
factors.summary: US-China summit ongoing (Trump-Xi talks in Beijing), some de-escalation messaging on Taiwan and trade. Middle East tensions remain (Iran, Yemen POWs), but no acute new shocks. Safety bid is muted.

factors.name: Central bank gold buying
factors.summary: India tightening gold import rules to defend rupee amid Middle East war, but no new large CB buying announcements in this window. Structural bid remains but not a fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Real yields vs nominal yields spread
factors.summary: April CPI and PPI both reaccelerated; core measures drifting further above Fed's 2% target (per UOB). Inflation not cooling, reducing the odds of rapid TIPS yield compression that would be gold-bullish.

factors.name: Gold technicals
factors.summary: News explicitly states gold 'struggles for direction below $4,700 amid stronger US Dollar and hawkish Fed outlook.' Price action weak; DXY in uptrend supporting USD, which inversely pressures gold.

factors.name: Risk-on equity momentum
factors.summary: Semiconductors surging (Nvidia +3.27%, Broadcom +4.14%), AI IPO (Cerebras +82% indicated), Cisco up 15%. Equities rallying hard; rotation out of gold/safe-havens into growth/tech is underway.

screening.narrative: Gold is under heavy pressure from a powerful confluence of hawkish Fed sentiment, surging US Dollar, and strong US economic data (retail sales, equity records, reduced recession fears). April inflation prints reaccelerated (CPI/PPI beats expected), pushing back any near-term Fed cut narrative and supporting real yields at elevated levels. Risk sentiment is decisively 'on'—equities at record highs, tech and semiconductors rallying hard—which reduces gold's safe-haven appeal. The US-China summit has reduced acute geopolitical spillover risk, further dampening the safety bid. Gold is stuck below $4,700 with multiple headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers/FOMC decision if scheduled in next 72h (no explicit mention but monitor for data-dependent policy shifts)
Next CPI print (likely early June; current inflation backdrop is hawkish)
PCE & Core PCE data (inflation expectations driver)
NFP release (labor market health feeds Fed easing/tightening calculus)
Continued Trump-Xi summit readout (trade war/tariff risk management)
Treasury auction or 10-year yield moves (affects real yield regime)
screening.risk_warning: If inflation prints cool materially, Fed pivot dovish, or US-China tensions spike (Taiwan mishandling or tariff shock), gold could reverse sharply higher. Real yields are the true swing factor—any unexpected drop in TIPS yields would flip bias to BUY. Also watch for USD consolidation or reversal if equities stumble; sudden risk-off and USD haven demand could also support gold despite hawkish Fed backdrop. Confidence capped at 7 (not 8+) because major economic data and Fed communications can shift quickly, and gold's dual-driver nature (real yields + USD + risk sentiment) means regime flips are always possible within 48-72h.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD strengthening broadly: EUR/USD down 0.22% for third consecutive day, USD/CHF up 0.15%, AUD/USD weakening below 200-hour MA (0.7220 area), NZD/USD testing key support at 0.5918. Strong dollar directly headwinds gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: BoE's Pill calls for 'prompt but modest' rate hike, signaling global hawkish tilt. Fed's Schmid states 'continued inflation is the most pressing risk' and 'inflation remains too high.' Principal Asset's Shah warns investors underpricing prospect Fed may abandon rate-cut bias. Delayed easing environment supports higher real yields.

factors.name: US Economic Data
factors.summary: April retail sales met consensus (resilient consumer per multiple sources), showing economic strength that reduces dovish Fed probability. US business inventories rose 0.9% vs 0.8% estimate. Jobless claims showed only slight increase. Data supports 'higher for longer' rate narrative.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: Silver down 3.20% 'as rising US yields, hawkish Fed remarks dent demand' per FXStreet. German bond yields 'stay near peak on inflation concerns.' Rising yields increase opportunity cost of holding zero-yield gold.

factors.name: Inflation Trajectory
factors.summary: Treasury Sec Bessent sees 'substantial disinflation' ahead as energy prices may reverse, reducing safe-haven inflation-hedge demand for gold. However, BoE's Pill warns '2nd round effects of inflation may be stronger,' creating mixed medium-term picture but near-term disinflation narrative caps gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Xi warns Trump that mishandling Taiwan could lead to 'dangerous' place; US-Iran negotiations ongoing with uncertainty; Pentagon halts 4,000 troops to Poland amid NATO tensions; Cuba energy crisis and protests. However, Trump-Xi summit atmosphere appears constructive ('constructive, strategic and stable' framing), reducing immediate escalation premium.

factors.name: Oil Prices / Energy
factors.summary: Hormuz oil flows 'creep higher as more supertankers exit,' offering 'limited relief' but still largest supply disruption in history. Higher oil drains spending power per Fed's Schmid but Bessent expects reversal. Bloomberg notes 'dollar's link to oil most positive ever' - supports USD strength, which is gold-negative.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit record highs; Cisco up 15% on AI demand; tech entering 'networking supercycle'; Cerebras IPO indicated to surge 82%. Strong risk-on equity environment reduces safe-haven gold demand. Markets closing at record levels despite geopolitical backdrop.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens
factors.summary: USD acting as primary safe haven (strengthening across all pairs), competing directly with gold. CHF also dipping vs USD. Yen briefly rallied on intervention speculation (USD/JPY volatile 157.30-158.16 range) but failed to sustain, reducing broader safe-haven flows.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: India tightening gold import rules 'to defend the rupee amid Middle East war' per Bloomberg - suggests reduced physical demand from major buyer. No fresh PBoC or other central bank buying announcements in news window.

factors.name: Positioning / Profit-Taking
factors.summary: Gold 'struggles for direction below $4,700' and silver 'slides as investors take profits after recent rally' per FXStreet. Technical weakening after extended run-up; gold at elevated absolute levels invites profit-taking.

confirmation.narrative: Gold faces coordinated headwinds from a strengthening USD (up across all major pairs), rising yields driven by hawkish Fed rhetoric (Schmid emphasizing inflation risks, Shah warning on underpriced hawkishness), and record-high equity markets reducing safe-haven demand. While geopolitical tensions persist (Taiwan warnings, Iran negotiations, NATO strain), the constructive Trump-Xi summit tone and resilient US economic data (retail sales in-line, low jobless claims) support a 'higher-for-longer' rate environment that increases gold's opportunity cost. Treasury Sec Bessent's disinflation outlook and potential energy price reversal further cap upside. Technical profit-taking below $4,700 after recent rally adds near-term pressure.
catalysts_ahead: Trump-Xi summit concluding Friday (May 15) - any Taiwan/trade escalation or de-escalation
Continuation of Fed speaker circuit (any pushback on hawkish narrative)
US-Iran negotiation updates (Hormuz strait closure risk)
Weekly jobless claims and any inflation data revisions
India import rule implementation impact on physical demand
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Trump-Xi talks collapse with Taiwan crisis escalation, triggering flight-to-quality; (2) USD reverses sharply lower on dovish Fed surprise or geopolitical USD-negative shock; (3) Oil prices spike on Hormuz closure, reigniting inflation fears and safe-haven demand; (4) Equity markets correct sharply (VIX spike), rotating flows into gold. Break above $4,720 with volume would signal renewed momentum.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY climbed to two-week high on Thursday as traders ramp up expectations for elevated Fed rates. Strong USD is inversely correlated with gold and directly pressures XAUUSD lower.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Fed Governor Miran's resignation coupled with Warsh transition signals potential hawkish continuity. US economic data showed resilience (strong retail sales, jobless claims uptick modest), fueling expectations Fed may keep rates elevated longer, raising real yields and pressuring gold.

factors.name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
factors.summary: Nominal yields supported by hawkish Fed commentary and strong economic data; higher nominal yields compress gold's opportunity cost, particularly when real yields also firm.

factors.name: US Economic Data
factors.summary: Retail sales resilience and moderate labor market (jobless claims data) reinforce Fed hawkishness; gold typically underperforms when growth fears recede and rate-cut expectations decline.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Middle East tensions (Iran strikes, Jerusalem Day march) persist, but Trump-Xi Beijing meeting and tentative US-China de-escalation messaging (IMF constructive dialogue, Boeing deal, Iran oil purchases) reduce acute safe-haven demand. Risk appetite has shifted back to equities (Nasdaq/S&P 500 rally on tech, Cerebras IPO surge).

factors.name: Risk Sentiment Regime
factors.summary: Equities rallying sharply (tech mega-cap gains, IPO euphoria, Bitcoin up 2.5%, AI mania in full swing); risk-on environment crowds out gold's safe-haven appeal. When equities soar on growth/inflation upside, gold often underperforms.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Recent war-driven inflation surge noted by Bessent, but Treasury official commentary suggests 'substantial disinflation' ahead as oil prices may reverse; longer-term inflation expectations remain contained, removing tail-risk gold premiums.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No recent news on PBoC, RBI, or other major central bank gold purchases in this news window; structural bid remains but not activated today.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No specific GLD or IAU flow data in the news window; unable to assess institutional positioning changes.

factors.name: Oil Prices / Inflation Input
factors.summary: ECB's Stournaras warned high oil could force rate hike; oil remains elevated but no fresh shock. Energy sector (Energy Vault, Ford AI pivot) shows mixed technicals; oil-driven inflation risk is real but priced in.

screening.narrative: Gold broke down -0.35% in 4h and -0.72% in 24h, following yesterday's SELL signal at 7/10. The dominant theme is hawkish Fed surprise (Miran resignation, Warsh ascension, resilient US data) driving real yields higher and DXY to two-week highs. Simultaneously, US-China de-escalation (Trump-Xi talks, Boeing deal, IMF commentary) and explosive tech rally (Cerebras +108%, Nvidia +20% in 7d, S&P 500/Nasdaq rebounds) shift sentiment away from safe-haven demand toward equities and risk assets. This is PHASE 2 of the sell move—initiated, but not yet extended enough for capitulation.
catalysts_ahead: PCE inflation release (if scheduled within 72h) — will determine whether Fed maintains hawkish hold or pivots toward cuts
Fed Speaker commentary (Waller, Williams, Bowman ongoing speeches) — additional hawkish signals could extend DXY rally
Oil price volatility (Brent/WTI moves) — energy component of inflation expectations
US equity earnings season continuation — risk sentiment barometer; if tech euphoria cools, safe-haven flows return
China-US trade/geopolitical headlines — any tariff escalation or Taiwan escalation could flip risk sentiment sharply
screening.risk_warning: If equity rally stalls (tech valuation reset, profit-taking), safe-haven demand snaps back and gold re-rallies. Conversely, if Fed speakers turn dovish or economic data cools (ISM, NFP miss), the sell bias could extend. Additionally, fresh Middle East escalation or unexpected geopolitical shock could trigger immediate gold spike regardless of rates/DXY. Watch for ECB cracks (Stournaras rate-hike warning) — if EUR weakens sharply, EUR/USD could collapse and underpin gold via DXY strength paradoxically failing.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway (Phase 2); wait for retest of 4700–4715 resistance before shorting fresh. Alternatively, if gold rallies back toward that zone, use it as a short entry with tight stops above 4720. Given upcoming event risk, avoid aggressive size until post-catalyst clarity.
